definition: Assign molecules to scaffold groups, usually Bemis-Murcko scaffolds, and treat same-scaffold train examples as high-overlap. For SPECTRA, scaffold identity is best used as one axis among others rather than as the sole proxy for molecular novelty.
example_usage: python tabular_group_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --group-col scaffold

citations.title: UMAP-based clustering split for rigorous evaluation of AI models for virtual screening on cancer cell lines

evidence.summary: The paper describes scaffold splits as grouping molecules with the same core structure.
The paper warns that different scaffolds can still correspond to highly similar molecules, leading to optimistic performance.
The paper reports scaffold splits retain higher maximum train-neighbor similarity than Butina and UMAP splits.

limitations: Different scaffolds can be structurally and functionally similar.
Scaffold identity is binary and may miss smooth novelty gradients.
Scaffold definitions vary across cheminformatics toolkits.
quality_gates: Pair scaffold identity with a continuous structure-similarity audit.
Report toolkit and scaffold extraction settings.
Check whether test scaffolds still have high nearest-train fingerprint similarity.
